title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Stats Cgroups Disk
summary: A service restarts intermittently with no useful application error while disk, memory, network, and health evidence disagree. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker stats cgroups disk increment in layered Docker incident evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-stats-cgroups-disk — Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-observability-debugging-2
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker observability debugging 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      baseline: ps-inspect-events-logs-stats
      correlation: release-digest-and-container-id
      mutation-policy: reproduce-before-exec
      diagnostic-image: separately-reviewed
      conclusion: first-causal-layer
    verify: docker inspect failed-api --format json && docker logs --timestamps failed-api
    failure: Reject interactive mutation as diagnosis, unbounded log collection, high-cardinality labels, or a restart presented as root-cause evidence.
    repair: Preserve state and timestamps, compare desired with observed configuration, isolate one layer, reproduce cleanly, and retain the causal regression.
